title: Modi-Starmer G7 Bilateral Advances India-UK Vision 2035 Across Five Strategic Pillars
lede: Prime Ministers Narendra Modi and Keir Starmer met on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in Evian to review progress on the India-UK Vision 2035 framework, signalling sustained momentum across trade, defence, climate, technology, and education partnerships.

sections.context: The bilateral meeting on 16 June 2026 between Modi and Starmer represents a continuation of high-level engagement initiated through reciprocal Prime Ministerial visits in 2025. The meeting occurred within the G7 Summit framework in Evian, France, underscoring India's elevated position in discussions among major developed economies. The leaders reviewed progress across all five pillars of Vision 2035—trade and economic growth, defence and security, climate action and green energy, technology and innovation, and education and people-to-people ties—indicating a comprehensive rather than sectoral approach to the bilateral relationship. The anticipated early entry into force of the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) represents the most tangible commercial outcome, while the expansion of UK university campuses in Indian cities signals institutional deepening of educational ties.
sections.threads: This meeting connects to the broader [[india-gb-india-uk-strategic-partnership-deepens-ceta-defence-roa-2026|India-UK strategic partnership trajectory that has accelerated significantly since Britain's post-Brexit recalibration]]. The emphasis on the Technology Security Initiative and the AI Impact Summit in Delhi earlier in 2026 indicates that [[india-gb-india-uk-strategic-partnership-enters-high-tech-phase-a-2026|India-UK ties are entering a high-technology phase]] focused on emerging domains. The education expansion—with University of Liverpool establishing a campus in Bengaluru and York and Bristol setting up in Mumbai—demonstrates how the relationship is moving beyond traditional government-to-government channels into institutional networks that sustain long-term people-to-people connectivity. The bilateral's occurrence at the G7 also signals India's integration into Western multilateral forums at a level that permits substantive bilateral time with major powers.
sections.signal: Analysts should monitor whether the Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement enters into force as anticipated; timing and any final conditions will indicate the trajectory of commercial ties. The Technology Security Initiative's further development warrants close observation, particularly regarding technology transfer protocols, cybersecurity cooperation, and whether UK firms gain preferential access to Indian digital and AI sectors. University campus development should be tracked as an indicator of sustained soft power investment; delays or cancellations would signal cooling ties. Within the next 6-18 months, expect announcements on joint defence projects or expanded security cooperation, given the inclusion of 'defence and security' as a primary pillar. The next critical juncture will likely be high-level visits or the conclusion of specific sectoral agreements under Vision 2035, which would demonstrate whether the framework remains aspirational or translates into binding institutional arrangements.
